strengths: 18+ years of fullstack experience with recent TypeScript/Node.js/React product work (Optilyz, RIDE, re:cinq), well beyond the 6-year requirement
Hands-on AI agent development at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks — a rare, explicitly required skill
Deep testing and craftsmanship culture: TDD/CI/CD/trunk-based practice, authored test libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), taught Selenium/TDD courses
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere on the résumé, despite it being in the primary stack
Kotlin absent and Java exposure is dated (robotic arms at Altom, 2016-2018); Python not listed among skills
Frontend depth leans on Ember.js across multiple roles; React evidence is limited to one position (Optilyz)

key_factors.factor: Missing Nest.js and Kotlin from primary/secondary stack

response.justification: Against the role's core competencies: fullstack TypeScript/Node — strong, evidenced by pricing-platform work at Optilyz using TypeScript, React, AWS CDK, and MongoDB; AI agent development — directly evidenced by MCP code generators and agentic-workflow frameworks at re:cinq; cloud/containers — evidenced by AWS CDK, Docker, and Kubernetes deployment of GISCollective; databases — MongoDB and MySQL both present; testing — evidenced by TDD practice at RIDE and authorship of two testing libraries; security/auth — no explicit evidence, only inferable from platform work; Nest.js and Kotlin — absent from the résumé. The gaps are framework-level rather than fundamental, and an engineer with this architecture and testing background typically absorbs Nest.js quickly, but the missing primary-stack evidence caps the score below the top band.
